revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24699703697|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Fukagawa jinja]][[ja:深川神社]] {{Expand Japanese|深川神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Fukagawa Shrine | native_name = 深川神社 | image = Hukagawajinja.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Fukagawa Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.227941|137.102684}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Fukagawa Shrine'''|深川神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province|ja|尾張国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11465292|1=List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province}} ** Series ordinal: 86 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039529|ltq=Q135039529|qid=Q135039529|1=Hitsushino Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135039530|ltq=Q135039530|qid=Q135039530|1=Kahashimano Shrine (Q135039530)}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Seto, Aichi|lt=Seto|ar|سيتو (آيتشي)|azb|ستو، آیچی|ca|Seto|ceb|Seto (lungsod)|ce|Сето (Айти)|ckb|سێتۆ، ئایچی|cs|Seto|de|Seto (Aichi)|en|Seto, Aichi|envoyage|Seto|eo|Seto (Ajĉio)|es|Seto (Aichi)|fa|ستو، آیچی|fi|Seto (Aichi)|fr|Seto|gl|Seto|hy|Սետո|id|Seto, Aichi|it|Seto|ja|瀬戸市|javoyage|瀬戸市|ko|세토시|lt|Setas (Aičis)|mg|Seto|mzn|ستو، آیچی|nl|Seto (Aichi)|no|Seto|pl|Seto (miasto)|pnb|سیٹو|pt|Seto|ro|Seto|ru|Сето (Айти)|sh|Seto|sr|Сето|sv|Seto|tg|Сето|tl|Seto, Aichi|tr|Seto|tt|Сето (Айти)|uk|Сето (Айті)|ur|سیتو، ایچی|uz|Seto (Aichi)|vi|Seto, Aichi|war|Seto, Aichi|zh_classical|瀨戶市|zh_min_nan|Seto-chhī|zh_yue|瀨戶|zh|瀨戶市|qid=Q499493|1=Seto, Aichi}} * {{ill|Q514506|ltq=Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} * {{ill|Yamada district, Owari|lt=Yamada district, Owari|ja|山田郡 (尾張国)|zh|山田郡 (尾張國)|qid=Q11470699|1=Yamada district, Owari}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.227941|137.102684}} == image (P18) == * Hukagawajinja.JPG == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181426 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q90139226|ltq=Q90139226|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * ふかがわじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5180005008933 == Commons category (P373) == * Fukagawa jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '〒489-0076 愛知県瀬戸市深川町11', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛知県瀬戸市深川町', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Fukagawa jinja}} [[JA:深川神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11560019}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Fukagawa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Seto Fukagawa jinja ac (1).jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = 〒489-0076
{{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Seto|Q499493|qid=Q499493|1=Seto, Aichi}}11 Fukagawa-chō |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto
Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto
Amatsu-Hikone-no-Mikoto
Ikatsu-Hichi-ne-no-Mikoto and others |社格 = {{ill|lt=Junior Third Rank|Q11071123|qid=Q11071123|1=Junior Third Rank}} Upper
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|qid=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 2 ({{ill|lt=771|Q29950|qid=Q29950|1=771}}){{Cite news|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131212001931/http://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/aichi/20131208/CK2013120802000053.html|title=First torii to be restored next spring after 14 years: Seto Fukagawa Shrine|newspaper =Chunichi Shimbun|date=2013-12-08|accessdate=2013-12-08}} |別名 = {{ill|lt=Hachiōji|Q11391544|qid=Q11391544|1=Hachiōji (disambiguation)}}
Hachiōji-sha |例祭 = October 15 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Fukagawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Fukagawa Shrine''' (ふかがわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Fukagawa-chō (Seto)|Q99541288|qid=Q99541288|lt=Fukagawa-chō|1=Fukagawa-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Seto|Q499493|qid=Q499493|1=Seto, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} identified with "Fukagawa Shrine" in Yamada District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. === History === Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|qid=Q184963}}, it was called '''Hachiōji''' and served as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|qid=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of {{ill|lt=Seto Village|Q119658043|qid=Q119658043|lt=Seto Village|1=Seto}}. Shrine tradition says it was founded in {{ill|lt=771|Q29950|qid=Q29950|1=771}} by inviting {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsukami|1=UNKNOWN}} deities. There is a horizontal-chamber {{ill|lt=round kofun|Q11394747|qid=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} in the precincts. {{ill|lt=Amano Nobukage|Q6535030|qid=Q6535030|1=Amano Sadakage}} identified it as the Yamada District "Fukagawa Shrine" of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and this became the accepted view. According to '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Owari Junkōki|1=UNKNOWN}},' there was a stream called Irikawa east of the shrine, formerly called "Fukagawa." There are almost no alternate views against identifying this shrine as Fukagawa Shrine, and scholars such as {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|qid=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} also support this. On divine rank, the '{{ill|lt=Owari Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|qid=Q11041830|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō}}' has "Junior Third Upper Fukagawa Tenjin" in the Jōji manuscript and "Junior Third Fukagawa Tenjin" in the Genki manuscript. On a temple bell once at this shrine's {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|qid=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} with the inscription {{ill|lt=Eikyō|Q1193059|qid=Q1193059|1=Eikyō}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1438|Q6377|qid=Q6377|1=1438}}), appears: "Seto Village in Yamada District, Owari; Ise Amaterasu Daijingū, Hakusan Myōri Kangen, this is the Hachiōji bell ... great donor Zuiryū Shūhaku," suggesting it was already called "Hachiōji" in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Muromachi period|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is said that when {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|qid=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}} visited during falconry, he was nearly attacked by an assassin of {{ill|lt=Saitō Tatsuoki|Q2880879|qid=Q2880879|1=Saitō Tatsuoki}} of Mino, but escaped thanks to the chief priest's quick action, and in thanks received 75 koku of offering rice. The oldest surviving {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|qid=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} is from {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1596|Q6785|qid=Q6785|1=1596}}), said to date from rebuilding at the current site after a fire. Several other 17th-century munafuda also survive. In the early-Edo '{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|qid=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}},' it reads "Seto Village ... Hachiōji ... formerly tax-exempt." The Ninomiya family served as {{ill|lt=shrine priests|Q11589454|qid=Q11589454|1=Shinkan (official)}} from old times, and '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Owari Junkōki|1=UNKNOWN}}' records "priest Ninomiya Jibū-dayū." There are three {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|qid=Q234731|1=Torii}}: first torii (approach entrance), second torii (precinct entrance), and third torii (in front of haiden). The first torii was built in {{ill|lt=1915|Q2084|qid=Q2084|1=1915}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 4), removed in {{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|qid=Q1985|1=2000}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 12) due to collapse risk, and rebuilt in {{ill|lt=2014|Q1999|qid=Q1999|1=2014}} (Heisei 26). The precincts were sometimes used as an entertainment venue. On {{ill|lt=February 28|Q2362|qid=Q2362|1=February 28}}, {{ill|lt=1943|Q18623|qid=Q18623|1=1943}} (Shōwa 18), a fire broke out at Ishibashi acrobat troupe grounds there, killing five spectators and injuring 29{{Cite book |和書 |editor=Nichigai Associates editorial dept. |title=Japan Disaster History Encyclopedia 1868-2009 |publisher=Nichigai Associates |year=2010-09-27 |page=61 |isbn=9784816922749}}. === Cultural properties === ; {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|qid=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} * Ceramic {{ill|lt=komainu|Q20155846|qid=Q20155846|1=Komainu}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kougei/kunisitei/0428.html |title=Ceramic Komainu, Honden |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |accessdate=2013-05-27}} - A {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} work. Shrine tradition says this is Japan's oldest ceramic komainu, made by Seto pottery ancestor {{ill|lt=Katō Kagemasa|Q11399278|qid=Q11399278|1=Kagemasa Kato}} (Shirōzaemon, Tōshirō). ; Seto City tangible cultural properties * Honden{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-03-28 |url=http://www.city.seto.aichi.jp/docs/2011012500028/ |title=Fukagawa Shrine Honden |publisher=Seto City |accessdate=2013-05-27}} - built in the {{ill|lt=Bunka era|Q1373146|qid=Q1373146|lt=Bunka period|1=Bunka}} of the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, by {{ill|lt=Tatekawa Washirō|Q65277448|qid=Q65277448|1=Tatekawa Washirō}} of {{ill|lt=Suwa region|Q11631849|qid=Q11631849|lt=Suwa|1=Suwa Region}}. * Tōhiko Shrine (honden, heiden, haiden, tsuiji wall) * {{ill|lt=Bonshō|Q8192099|qid=Q8192099|1=Bonshō}}{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-03-28 |url=http://www.city.seto.aichi.jp/docs/2011012500011/ |title=Bonshō with Eikyō inscription |publisher=Seto City |accessdate=2013-05-27}} - made in the {{ill|lt=Eikyō|Q1193059|qid=Q1193059|1=Eikyō}} era of the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|qid=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, evidence of {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shūgō|Q2279088|qid=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}. * Oribe lantern{{Cite web|和書|date=2011-03-28 |url=http://www.city.seto.aichi.jp/docs/2011012400090/ |title=Oribe lantern |publisher=Seto City |accessdate=2013-05-27}} === Annual rites === * Jan 1 - New Year's Day festival * Jan 5 - First Ebisu festival * Feb 3 - Setsubun festival * Feb 17 - Kinen festival * 3rd Sunday of April - Tōso festival * Jun 30 - Nagoshi great purification * Oct 15 - Annual festival * Nov 23 - Niiname festival * Dec 31 - Great purification === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Seto|Q499493|qid=Q499493|1=Seto, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Fukagawa-chō (Seto)|Q99541288|qid=Q99541288|lt=Fukagawa-chō|1=Fukagawa-chō}} 11 ; Access * About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Owari-Seto Station|Q7114294|qid=Q7114294|1=Owari Seto Station}} on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Seto Line|Q6810422|qid=Q6810422|lt=Seto Line|1=Meitetsu Seto Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|qid=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related item === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tōso Festival|1=UNKNOWN}} - festival honoring pottery ancestor Tōshirō; discounted Seto ware market held at venues in Seto. === External link === * [http://seto-fukagawashrine.com/ Fukagawa Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Fukagawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Fukagawa Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:瀬戸市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Seto, Aichi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]]
